front: Why? Because I don't want to.
For this reason, I don't want to go.
back: Por esta razón, no quiero ir.

front: I'm buying it because of you.
I will stay for three days.
I would stay for three days.
I would stay three days, but I have to work.
back: Me quedaría tres días, pero tengo que trabajar.

front: I have come because of you.
I would change this one for that one.
Would you change these for those? (fem)

hint: object sticks on end of 'to' form

front: Why wouldn't you change these for those?
back: ¿Por qué no cambiarías estas por esas?

front: It is because of you that we have come.
back: Es por ti que hemos venido.

front: It's a way of doing it.
I don't believe in arguing in this way.
back: No creo en discutir de esta forma.
hint: de esta forma = in this way

front: We have spoken to resolve it.
We have spoken to try to resolve it.
I am here to see you.
I've called you to speak about it.

back: Estoy tomando un curso de español.

front: I was taking a Spanish course.
back: Estaba tomando un curso de español.

front: I couldn't do it because I didn't have the time.
back: No podía hacerlo porque no tenía el tiempo.

front: He wanted to dine with me.
He wanted to dine with me, but I had already dined.
back: Él quería cenar conmigo, pero yo ya había cenado.

front: He was looking for it because I had lost it.
back: Lo buscaba porque yo lo había perdido.

front: I would like to stay for three days.
back: Me gustaría quedarme por tres días.
No me quedo todos los días.

front: I don't believe in arguing about it.
In this way it is possible.
It's because of you that I'm tired. (fem)
back: Es por ti que estoy cansada.

front: I have come because of her.
We are taking a Spanish course.
back: Estamos tomando un curso de español.

front: I'm tired of dancing on Mondays.
back: Estoy cansada de bailar los lunes.
hint: after de: 'to' form · 'on Mondays' = los lunes

front: I would have called you to explain it.
hint: habría + part · para = in order to
